reforms.description: The reform of the unemployment-insurance system has directed more funds towards training for the unemployed, while reducing the generosity of the system by reducing the maximum duration of benefits and increasing the minimum period of work required to qualify for benefits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in training for the unemployed enhances human capital, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect as part of the unemployment insurance system.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the funding allocation within the unemployment insurance system, impacting training opportunities.
reforms.source_quote: Under the Labour Force Development Strategy, funding for training was raised from C$ 0.5 billion in 1990 to C$ 2 billion in 1992.

reforms.description: The Canadian Government has initiated the establishment of Centres for Excellence to link top researchers from universities with major corporations, aiming to improve the effectiveness of existing R&D support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Linking researchers with corporations enhances knowledge transfer and innovation, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between academia and industry, improving R&D outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has initiated a number of programmes to address these problems. Among these is the establishment of the Centres for Excellence, which link up top researchers of universities and major corporations.
